---
title: "RAG Pipeline"
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
---

## Building a knowledge base

* A **knowledge base** is a repository of documents or structured data used during retrieval.
* The pre-requisite of implementing this is that we have "relevant data" accessible:
  1. Databases like: SQL, NoSQL, or Vector Database
  2. APIs: internal or external

## Full pipeline: from data ingestion to generation

A typical retrieval workflow looks like this:

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    %% Text processing
    A[/Texts/] --> B
    subgraph "📥 Text processing"
        B[Loaders]
        B --> B2[/Documents/]
        B2 --> C[Splitters]
        C --> D[/Chunks/]
    end

    %% Embedding & storage
    subgraph "🔢 Embedding & storage"
        D --> E[Embedding model]
        E --> F[/Vectors/]
        F --> G[(Vector stores)]
    end

    %% Retrieval
    H[/Query/] --> I
    subgraph "🔍 Retrieval"
        I["Embedding model"]
        I --> J[/Vector/]
        J --> K[Retrievers]
        K -- Vector --> G
        G -- Chunks --> K
        K --> L[/Context/]
    end

    %% Generation
    P[/Answer/]
    subgraph "Generation"
        L -- Augment --> O[LLM]
    end
    O --> P
```

Each component is modular: you can swap loaders, splitters, embeddings, or vector stores without rewriting the app’s logic.

# Building blocks

## 1. Loaders

[Document loaders](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/document_loaders) Ingest data from external sources (Google Drive, Slack, Notion, etc.), returning standardized [`Document`](https://reference.langchain.com/python/langchain-core/documents/base/Document) objects.


```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  S[/"Sources<br>(Google Drive, Slack, Notion, etc.)"/] --> L[Document Loaders]
  L --> A[/Documents/]
```

## 2. Splitters

[Text splitters](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/splitters) Break large docs into smaller chunks that will be retrievable individually.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[/Documents/] --> B[Splitter]
  B --> C1[/Chunk 1/]
  B --> C2[/Chunk 2/]
  B --> C3[/Chunk 3/]
```

**Why do we split?..**

## A. Hard Limit: Context Window

- Many documents (legal contracts, technical manuals, books) are significantly larger than any LLM's processing window
- Practical limits are often much less than theoretical limits.
- By splitting a 500-page manual, we can selectively retrieve relevant context to answer a specific query.

## B. Soft Limit: "Lost in the Middle" Phenomena

[Amazon Science Research shows](https://www.amazon.science/publications/context-length-alone-hurts-llm-performance-despite-perfect-retrieval) that LLMs are best at recalling information located at the very beginning or the very end of a prompt; **but not when it is buried in the middle of a massive block of text**.

## C. Cost and Latency

- Say we want to know a specific date in a 100MB file, sending the whole file is expensive and slow.
- Every token sent to an LLM costs **money** and takes **time** to process.
- Sending three 500-token chunks is nearly instantaneous and costs a fraction of the price.

## 3. Embedding Models

[Embedding models](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/text_embedding) An embedding model turns text into a vector of numbers so that texts with similar meaning land close together in that vector space.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  D1[Embedding Model]
  D2[Embedding Model]
  D3[Embedding Model]
  C1[/Chunk 1/] --> D1 --> E1[/Vector 1/]
  C2[/Chunk 2/] --> D2 --> E2[/Vector 2/]
  C3[/Chunk 3/] --> D3 --> E3[/Vector 3/]
```

## 4. Stores

[Vector stores](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/vectorstores/) Specialized databases for storing and searching embeddings.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    S[(Vector Store)]
    E1[/Vector 1/] --> S
    E2[/Vector 2/] --> S
    E3[/Vector 3/] --> S
```

## 5. Retrievers

[Retrievers](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/retrievers/) A retriever is an interface that returns documents given an unstructured query.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    R[Retriever] --> S[(Vector Store)]
    S --> R
    R --> C1[/Chunk 14/]
    R --> C2[/Chunk 8/]
    R --> C3[/Chunk 62/]
```

## Embeddings + Keywords Search

- Search engines don't just rely on embeddings.
- [ChromaDB](https://docs.trychroma.com/docs/overview/introduction) supports Full-text and Regex Search alongside embeddings.
- **Hybrid search** combines keyword and semantic search to improve search relevance.
- [Reciprocal rank fusion (RRF)](https://plg.uwaterloo.ca/%7Egvcormac/cormacksigir09-rrf.pdf) is a method for combining multiple result sets with different relevance indicators into a single result set.

## Chunking

Two possible chunking approaches:

1. chunk the `DoclingDocument` itself
2. export `DoclingDocument` to Markdown then chunk as a post-processing step

## Hierarchical Chunker

- The `HierarchicalChunker` implementation uses the document structure information from the [`DoclingDocument`](https://docling-project.github.io/docling/concepts/docling_document/) to create one chunk for each individual detected document element.
- It also takes care of attaching all relevant document metadata, including headers and captions.

## Hybrid Chunker

```python
from docling.chunking import HybridChunker
```

- Apply tokenization-aware refinements on top of document-based hierarchical chunking.
- Control how table headers are handled:
  - **`repeat_table_header`** (default: `True`): When enabled, table headers are repeated at the beginning of each chunk when a table spans multiple chunks.
  - **`omit_header_on_overflow`** (default: `False`): for handling wide tables.

## Docling Loader using Hybrid Chunking

`HybridChunker` should be aligned to the embedding model tokenizer

```python
from docling.chunking import HybridChunker
from langchain_docling.loader import ExportType

embed_model_id = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
tokenizer = HuggingFaceTokenizer(
  tokenizer=AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(embed_model_id)
)

converter = DocumentConverter(...)

loader = DoclingLoader(
    file_path=file_path,
    converter=converter,
    export_type=ExportType.DOC_CHUNKS,
    chunker=HybridChunker(tokenizer=tokenizer),
)
```

## Context expansion

- Relevant chunks may lack information necessary for producing a fully grounded response.
- Docling's preserves document structure!
- Key idea: include adjacent chunks (from the same section) before generating the final answer.

## Key Takeaways

* **Retrieval solves two LLM limits**:
  - Static (frozen) knowledge and finite context.
  - Fetching relevant external information at query time is the foundation of **RAG** (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).
* **The retrieval pipeline is modular**:
  - **Load** (document loaders) → **Split** (text splitters) → **Embed** (embedding models) → **Store** (vector stores) → **Retrieve** (retrievers).
  - You can swap any component without changing the rest of the app.
* **Splitting matters** for:
  - Staying within context windows.
  - Avoiding "lost in the middle" (LLMs recall start/end of input better).
  - Lowering cost and latency.

## References

- https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/retrieval
